signInInformation.title: Changes to Login Process

description.text:
We have merged the login process for EuroLeagueTV and EuroLeague ID – now a single account lets you access both, plus our Fantasy Challenge game.

**If you previously just had a EuroLeagueTV account, please use the forgot password function to activate your new Euroleague ID account.**

If you previously just had a Euroleague ID account, congratulations! You now also have access to a free tier of videos on EuroLeagueTV.\*

**If you are accessing our Fantasy Challenge via our partner's website, please be informed that our partners do not process, collect nor store any user data of participating users as stated in our Privacy Policy**

signInInformation.asteriskText: *EuroLeagueTV is available worldwide excluding Spain, France, Italy and Monaco.

accountDeletionInfo.text:
Before continuing to delete your EuroLeague ID account, please bear in mind the following:

If you play the Euroleague Fantasy Challenge, you may want to delete your team there first, as it will be more difficult to do that if your account no longer exists. You can do that at [euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account](http://euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account).

club.info: Virtus Bologna, which began as a gymnastics club in 1879, formed its first professional basketball team in the late 1920s and proceeded to win championships consistently for more than half a century. It won four consecutive Italian League titles from 1945 to 1948 and then added more in 1955 and 1956. After a two-decade drought, Virtus claimed four more Italian League crowns between 1976 and 1984, the last coming with an Italian Cup victory for the club's first double. In 1990, Virtus won its first European title, the Saporta Cup. That came nine years after reaching the 1981 EuroLeague final. Virtus captured its first EuroLeague crown in 1998 when it beat AEK Athens in Barcelona. Coach Ettore Messina led Virtus to a second EuroLeague championship in 2001 with Manu Ginobili chosen as Finals MVP. Virtus lost in the 2002 EuroLeague final and soon after suffered financial troubles, which saw it slip to the Italian second division. On the rebound, it won the BCL in 2019 and the EuroCup in 2022 to secure its return to the EuroLeague. In 2023, Virtus won the Italian Cup, and in the 2023-24 campaign, was one win away from qualifying for the EuroLeague Playoffs. Last season Virtus struggled in the EuroLeague, but was successful domestically and, behind Coach Dusko Ivanovic, stormed through the Italian League playoffs to win another Italian crown.

heroMedia.title: Isaac Bonga’s career night leads Partizan past Virtus
heroMedia.summary: A career-best PIR from Isaac Bonga and clutch scoring from Carlik Jones fuel Partizan’s statement win in Bologna

linkedIds.text: Virtus Bologna v Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (2026-03-11T19:45:00.000Z)

content.text:
[Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/partizan-mozzart-bet-belgrade/par/) has been playing better in recent games and it seemed only a matter of time before registering a significant road victory. After a home win against Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens and narrow losses - 4 points or less - against Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv, Real Madrid and Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul, Partizan topped [Virtus Bologna](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/virtus-segafredo-bologna/vir/) 82-88 in Round 31 and showed a lot of good along the way. 

No-one offered more energy and was more effective than versatile forward [Isaac Bonga](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/isaac-bonga/006760/), who was the best player on the floor. He finished the game with 16 points, 11 rebounds, 5 assists, 2 blocks and 6 fouls drawn for a career-high PIR of 34. It was his fifth career double-double. [Tonye Jekiri](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/tonye-jekiri/009733/) took advantage of his extended playing time due to [Bruno Fernando](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/bruno-fernando/013667/)'s foul trouble to post 10 points, 6 rebounds, 2 assists and 1 steal.

"[It was] a physical game, a hustle game, just making sure they don't get energy from the little things like offensive rebounds, 50-50 balls, and I think in the second half, we just did a better job," Bonga said. "I think we just adjusted. We adjusted in the second half, played definitely way more calmly, made sure that we secured the ball, and the good thing is that we secured the win in the end."

content.title: Virtus Bologna-Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade | Round 31 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
There was more good news for coach [Joan Penarroya](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/partizan-mozzart-bet-belgrade/par/penarroya-joan)'s team. [Carlik Jones](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/carlik-jones/013369/) confirmed his good form after missing 23 regular season games due to injury. He had 19 points in 26 minutes and stepped up big down the stretch. With the game on the line, Jones buried a turnaround jumper and added a backdoor layup off a Virtus timeout to seal the outcome, 74-84, in the next-to-last minute. 

"I'm blessed to be back. I'm so glad to be back. I miss them [Partizan fans] as much as they miss me, having all these fans traveling. We have always been blessed to have that kind of thing. And they helped us tonight," Jones said after the game, pointing out his team's competitiveness. 

"We are here to compete still. A lot of things that's going on, no matter what, we are still going to play hard. We are still going to try to defend our home court, and try to win on the road as well. And we are going to play this thing out."

BRAVOOO CRNO-BELI!🖤🤍#KKPartizan #EuroLeague pic.twitter.com/h5SgmTsghv

— KK Partizan Mozzart Bet (@PartizanBC) March 11, 2026

Partizan played the role of spoiler. Despite not having significant postseason chances, it beat a team that is ahead of it in the standings. Virtus took a step backwards in its goal to reach the Play-In Showdown. After the game, Coach Penarroya praised his team for its defense and hustle.

"In the last month, in the last five weeks, we competed very well. I think we deserve to win the game tonight, and I think we deserved to win other games in other weeks. We played better in the last quarter, and especially we controlled our defense better in the second half," Coach Penarroya said.

"We played better defense, especially in the second half, we controlled their transition play. We improved in defensive rebounds. Only our turnovers were a little bit of a problem, but I think we controlled the pace and we deserved to win."  

Partizan plays six of its eight remaining games in Belgrade, five at home and one at archrival Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade.

linkedId.text: Virtus Bologna v Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (2026-03-11T19:45:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Isaac Bonga’s career night leads Partizan past Virtus
articleMetadata.description: Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade earned a standout 82–88 road win over Virtus Bologna in Round 31, powered by Isaac Bonga’s career-high PIR 34 performance and Carlik Jones’s clutch fourth-quarter scoring. Despite limited postseason prospects, Partizan impressed with improved defense, hustle and composure, handing Virtus a setback in its Play-In pursuit. With six of their final eight games in Belgrade, Partizan looks to build on its strongest stretch of the season.

heroMedia.title: Carsen Edwards made Virtus history against Real Madrid
heroMedia.summary: He broke the club's single-season scoring record

content.text:
[Virtus Bologna](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/virtus-segafredo-bologna/vir/) may have [lost at Real Madrid](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/news/real-madrid-rolls-on-as-mario-hezonja-delivers-in-home-win-over-virtus/) on Thursday night, but [Carsen Edwards](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/carsen-edwards/011987/) wrote his name into the club's history books.

Edwards – who finished second in the race for the Alphonso Ford Top Scorer trophy last season with FC Bayern Munich – entered Round 30 needing seven points to become Virtus's highest scorer in a single EuroLeague season. He erupted for 32 points, his third-highest tally of the season, to reach 519 points and claim the new Virtus single-season record.

content.title: Carsen Edwards's top scoring plays in 2025-26
content.description: The best scoring plays of Virtus Bologna star Carsen Edwards through the first 30 rounds of the 2025-26 EuroLeague season.

content.text:
The previous record belonged to [Marco Belinelli](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/marco-belinelli/bcn/), who scored 493 points in the 2023-24 season. However, Edwards still has work to do to match his own personal record of 696 points in the 2024-25 regular season (713 total after playing in Bayern's first play-in game before being injured). In his rookie EuroLeague season with Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul in 2022-23 he scored 271 points, followed by 390 in his first Bayern campaign in 2023-24.

Now in his fourth EuroLeague season, the 27-year-old has established himself as one of the league's deadliest scorers. He has recorded nine career 30-point games, with eight coming in the last two seasons. He set his [career high of 36 points in Round 5 this season against LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/news/carsen-edwards-is-back-doing-carsen-edwards-things/), and came close with 35 points against former side Fenerbahce in Round 23. Before this season, his best was 34 points in Bayern's German derby win at ALBA Berlin in January 2025.

articleMetadata.title: Carsen Edwards made Virtus history against Real Madrid
articleMetadata.description: Virtus Bologna may have lost at Real Madrid on Thursday night, but Carsen Edwards wrote his name into the club's history books.

heroMedia.title: Real Madrid rolls on as Mario Hezonja delivers in home win over Virtus
heroMedia.summary: The Croatian forward was lethal when the chips were down to steer the hosts passed a plucky Italian rival

content.text:
[Real Madrid](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/real-madrid/mad/) notched its third straight win and improved to 19-11 in the EuroLeague after [Mario Hezonja](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/mario-hezonja/003108/) poured 8 of his 19 points in crunch time to engineer a 92-84 home victory over [Virtus Bologna](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/virtus-segafredo-bologna/vir/) on Thursday.

With the teams tied 77-77, Hezonja sank a couple of three-pointers and two free throws as Real Madrid hit top gear when it needed to and stayed firmly on course to reach the playoffs. Virtus, in contrast, saw its postseason hopes fade as the Italian side dropped to 13-17 despite a valiant solo effort from [Carsen Edwards](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/carsen-edwards/011987/).

The livewire guard netted 22 of his 32 points in the first half but fizzled out after the break under aggressive man-to-man defense from [Sergio Llull](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/sergio-llull/tgb/). Virtus erased an 11-point deficit and led 69-72 early in the fourth before Real Madrid found an extra gear down the stretch.

content.title: Real Madrid-Virtus Bologna | Round 30 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Virtus recovered from a halftime buzzer-beater by [Facundo Campazzo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/facundo-campazzo/005928/), who hit nothing but net off the glass from midcourt, as well as from a [Gaby Deck](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/gabriel-deck/009005/) three-pointer at the end of the third quarter. Edwards then exploded again early in the fourth but his one-man show was brought to an end by a ruthlessly efficient Real Madrid unit in a lop-sided finish to the game.    

“I am glad we stuck together and got the win,” a delighted Hezonja said after earning the man of the game accolade. “We were very solid defensively, they had a bit of a run but we were able to stop it and find some great options on offense.” 

Real Madrid sank 11-of-28 from three-point range after starting 0-of-5, allowing Edwards to dominate early. He scored 10 of Virtus' opening 17 points to give the Italian side a 14-21 lead. Real Madrid responded and led 46-40 at halftime, but the lead changed hands again before *Los Blancos* forced the final twist in a see-saw affair.  

“Virtus showed several times this season that they are capable of beating anyone,” Real Madrid head coach Sergio Scariolo said. “They are a really good team, especially when they shoot the ball like they did tonight most of the time. We did a better job on Edwards in the second half. You learn from games like this one that you’ve got to be on top until the very last possession.”

✌ ¡Doble MVP en el partido frente a la @VirtusBo!
✨ @waltertavares22 & @mariohezonja pic.twitter.com/H1wqTrk70G

— Real Madrid Basket (@RMBaloncesto) March 5, 2026

Big man [Walter Tavares](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/walter-tavares/005791/) added 14 points and blocked 5 shots for Real Madrid and Campazzo chipped in with 12, while Llull and Deck netted 9 each. [Luca Vildoza](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/luca-vildoza/007947/) sank 15 and [Derrick Alston](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/derrick-alston-jr--/014005/) scored 11 in defeat for Virtus, with [Alen Smailagic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/alen-smailagic/011051/) grabbing 11 boards in a tight tussle with Tavares.

Crucially, Real forced 15 turnovers to Virtus's 9, with Hezonja adding 6 boards and as many assists to his tally. 

“We expected a tough game but we managed to gain the upper hand in the second and fourth quarters to compensate for not dominating the boards,” Scariolo pointed out at the press conference. “Virtus is a team that never gives up and we showed character when they edged ahead early in the fourth quarter.”

Virtus won the battle of the boards 32-34 but Real Madrid stuck to its task and found a way to come out on top thanks to a solid team effort which cancelled out Edwards, who ran out of steam in the home straight.

articleMetadata.title: Real Madrid rolls on as Mario Hezonja delivers in home win over Virtus
articleMetadata.description: Real Madrid notched its third straight win and improved to 19-11 in the EuroLeague after Mario Hezonja poured 8 of his 19 points in crunch time to engineer a 92-84 home victory over Virtus Bologna on Thursday.

heroMedia.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out

content.text:
The extraordinary demand for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad shows no signs of slowing down, as all tickets made available during Phase 2 of public sales have officially sold out.

When Phase 2 opened on February 26 at 10:00 CET, fans once again responded in massive numbers. Following Phase 1, which saw more than one million fans connect to the ticketing platform and resulted in a complete sellout, the second allocation was also quickly exhausted, reaffirming the immense appeal of European basketball’s premier event.

The overwhelming global interest highlights the unique atmosphere and significance of the Final Four, with fans from across Europe and beyond eager to secure their place in Athens for the season’s ultimate showdown.

Fans who were unable to purchase tickets during General public tickets sale Phase 2 will still have an additional opportunity on February 26 as VIP tickets will be available starting at 14:00 CET.

Fans who were unable to secure tickets during Phase 1 will still have multiple opportunities to attend European basketball’s premier event. The second public ticket sales phase will open on February 26 at 10:00 CET. The third public ticket sales phase will open on March 17 at 10:00 CET, while the final sales phase will begin on April 20 at 10:00 CEST, coinciding with the conclusion of the Regular Season as teams continue their pursuit of a Final Four berth.

Each remaining phase will feature a limited allocation across multiple price categories, and high demand is expected once again.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

articleMetadata.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out
articleMetadata.description: The extraordinary demand for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad shows no signs of slowing down, as all tickets made available during Phase 2 of public sales have officially sold out.

heroMedia.title: Carsen Edwards seals Virtus’s valiant victory over visiting Barcelona
heroMedia.summary: Defense, depth and late‑game shot‑making carry Virtus after a sluggish start

content.text:
A back-and-forth battle was expected in Round 29, as [Virtus Bologna](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/virtus-segafredo-bologna/vir/) and [FC Barcelona](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/fc-barcelona/bar/) came off semifinal losses in domestic cup action and both teams needed to bounce back. Virtus had the final word, downing Barca 85-80  at home to improve to 13–16. After a slow start, Virtus played its best basketball in the second and third quarters, then held off Barca’s late rally behind an outstanding [Carsen Edwards](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/carsen-edwards/011987/). 

Barca leaned on two former Virtus stars from the jump. [Kevin Punter](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/kevin-punter/009862/) erupted for 12 first‑quarter points on 3-of-3 three‑point shooting. In his first visit to Virtus Arena since joining Barca, [Toko Shengelia](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/tornike-shengelia/001133/) stuffed the opening 10 minutes with 9 points on perfect shooting and 5 rebounds for a PIR of 14. Shengelia was honored before the game and, as always, put emotions aside once the ball went up. Across nearly four seasons with Virtus, he won an Italian League crown and the BKT EuroCup.

content.title: Virtus Bologna-FC Barcelona | Round 29 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Virtus then clamped down over the next 20 minutes, limiting Barca to 7-of-15 two‑point shooting (46.7%), 7 assists and 10 turnovers. At the same time, Virtus went 19-of-25 on twos (76%) in the second and third quarters, dished 14 assists, grabbed 7 steals and committed just 3 turnovers. [Luca Vildoza](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/luca-vildoza/007947/) reached halftime with 5 steals, tying a club mark that belonged to [Fabio Di Bella](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/fabio-di-bella/tgq/), who had 5 first‑half steals in a 101–91 home win over Union Olimpija on November 29, 2007.

Virtus led 68–57 after three and stretched the margin to 79–68 with just over 4 minutes left. A game of runs felt inevitable after a demanding weekend, and Barca delivered down the stretch: Shengelia and Punter combined for 10 points in a 0–12 burst that [Nico Laprovittola](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nicolas-laprovittola/006435/) capped with a floater for a 79-80 Barca lead in the penultimate minute. Just when a fourth straight defeat loomed for Virtus, Alen Smailagic drew a foul and sank both free throws. After a pair of defensive stops, Virtus put the ball in Edwards’s hands; he followed a lane floater with a step‑back jumper that sealed the outcome, 85–80, with 10.5 seconds left.

"It was a good win for us. I think I just try to move on and just try to play the best I can every game, so that's really what I'm focused on," Edwards said after the game. "I just play. That's all I do is just play to have fun."

What a 𝐓𝐄𝐀𝐌 ❤️‍🔥🤍🖤 pic.twitter.com/jUfSmuvd2h

— Virtus Bologna (@VirtusBo) February 25, 2026

Smailagic finished the game with season-highs of 19 points and a PIR of 21. After 10 consecutive single-digit scoring games and an injury, Smailagic has averaged 15.5 points in the last two games. 

"It feels amazing, especially after a couple of months that I didn't play as I should play. It's good to be back on track," Smailagic said. "We still believe we can get that [play-in] spot, so are just going to keep fighting and keep believing."

Edwards led the way with 22 points. Vildoza finished with 10 points, 7 assists and 5 steals. Punter was Barca's top scorer with 27 points, Shengelia added 16 and Laprovittola tallied 14 points and a career-high 12 assists. 

Virtus played three of its next four games on the road before a four-game home stretch. Barca returns to Italy to face EA7 Emporio Armani in Round 30 before hosting Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv in Round 31.

articleMetadata.title: Edwards seals Virtus’s valiant victory over visiting Barca
articleMetadata.description: After both teams entered Round 29 seeking a response to domestic‑cup exits, Virtus Bologna edged FC Barcelona 85–80 at home. The hosts flipped the game with a dominant second and third quarter, then survived Barca’s 12–0 late run. Carsen Edwards (22 pts) hit a floater and step‑back in the final minute to seal it, while Alen Smailagic posted season highs (19 pts, PIR 21) and Luca Vildoza added 10 pts, 7 ast, 5 stl. Kevin Punter (27 pts), Toko Shengelia (16) and Nico Laprovittola (14 pts, career‑high 12 ast) led Barca.

heroMedia.title: Power rankings: After the cups have gone
heroMedia.summary: Our experts offer up a suggestion for every team through 28 rounds

content.text:
Welcome to the latest edition of the EuroLeague Power Rankings. The list is the brainchild of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net)'s team of experts, which is made up of Geoff Gillingham, Javier Gancedo, Frankie Sachs and Igor Petrinovic. The rankings are not a prediction of what the final standings will look like, but rather where all 20 teams stand – in their humble opinions – today, after Round 28.

 

### 1. Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul (20-7)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Besiktas on Sunday to win the Turkish Cup

**The question we are asking:** Is Fenerbahce aware that not team to finish the regular season in first place under the current format has won the championship?

 

### 2. Olympiacos Piraeus (18-9)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Panathinaikos in the Greek Cup final on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Do we need to start worrying about the health of the Reds’ stars?

 

### 3. Real Madrid (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Baskonia in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can *Los Blancos* improve its defense to become a top contender?

### 4. Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Olympiacos to win the Greek Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Will the addition of Nigel Hayes-Davis be enough to put the Greens over the top?

 

### 5. Valencia Basket (18-10)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** How will Valencia shake its disappointment from not winning the Spanish Cup at home? 

 

### 6. FC Barcelona (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Barcelona in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** With Punter back in action, can Barça improve its consistency and stay in the top-six race?

 

### 7. Zalgiris Kaunas (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Rytas Vilnius in the Lithuanian Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** With six home games remaining and good momentum, is a top-six finish a realistic goal?

### 8. AS Monaco (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Le Mans to win the French Leaders Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After a turbulent stretch on and off the court, can this trophy galvanize Monaco and stabilize its EuroLeague push?

 

### 9. Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv (16-11)

**What they did last week:** Went 3-0 in the Israeli League

**The question we are asking:** Is Hapoel’s recent slide just a bump in the road or a sign that the team peaked too early?

 

### 10. Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Mega Belgrade to win the Serbian Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Jordan Nwora the star who can carry Zvezda to new heights? 

 

### 11. Dubai Basketball (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bosna Sarajevo in the ABA League

**The question we are asking:** Now that Dzanan Musa is back and in shape, can Dubai win enough games to make it to the Play-In Showdown?

### 12. Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (13-15)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bnei Herzliya to win the Israeli Cup on Thursday

**The question we are asking:** Is there enough time left in the season for Maccabi to climb to 10th place?

 

### 13. EA7 Emporio Armani Milan (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Tortona to win the Italian Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can Milan translate domestic momentum into EuroLeague consistency and revive its play-in hopes?

 

### 14. FC Bayern Munich (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bamberg in German Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What would this team have looked like with a full season under Coach Pesic?

 

### 15. Anadolu Efes Istanbul (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Besiktas in the Turkish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Efes destined to become a spoiler for a few teams in the playoff race?

### 16. Virtus Bologna (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Tortona in the Italian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What adjustments can Dusko Ivanovic make to halt Virtus’s slide – especially away from home?

 

### 17. Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Mega Belgrade in the Serbian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Can Joan Penarroya realign the locker room and restore Partizan’s competitive edge?

 

### 18. Paris Basketball (9-18)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bourg in the French Leader’s Cup quarterfinals on Friday

**The question we are asking:** What will it take for Paris to recapture its early-season identity and get Nadir Hifi back to his best?

 

### 19. Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After its Spanish Cup success and strong home performances, can Baskonia start winning consistently on the road?

 

### 20. LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (7-21)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Monaco in the French Cup quarterfinals on Tuesday

**The question we are asking:** With the postseason out of reach, which players are auditioning for their EuroLeague futures?

heroMedia.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
heroMedia.summary: Phase 1 general public ticket sale attracted over 1 million fans worldwide

content.text:
Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

The launch of Phase 2 coincides with the closing of the EuroLeague players’ market, ensuring that fans will have a clear picture of each team’s final roster.

All fans,  except those who have already purchased tickets during previous sales phases or pre-sales , will be eligible to access the February 26 general public sale starting at 10:00 CET. During this second sales window,  all fans will also have the opportunity to purchase VIP tickets starting at 14:00 CET .

Ticketing information is available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/). Fans must log in or create a EuroLeague ID account to complete their purchase. Once logged in, all available categories will be displayed. Due to expected high demand and to ensure fairness, each fan may purchase a maximum of two tickets.

For the first time, Final Four tickets are being released in multiple sales phases. This approach is designed to expand access for the EuroLeague’s growing global fan base, offering multiple opportunities to secure tickets,  whether fans plan to attend regardless of the qualified teams or prefer to wait until the competition reaches its decisive stages. All phases follow a fair access policy, meaning that fans who purchase tickets in one phase will not be eligible to participate in subsequent phases. This measure is designed to ensure broader access to the event.

Phase 1, held on February 11, experienced massive demand, as over a million fans connected to sold out all available tickets in less than five minutes, once again demonstrating the enormous appeal of the EuroLeague’s premier event.

The 2026 EuroLeague Final Four semifinals will be played on Friday, May 22, at 18:00 and 21:00 local time (17:00 and 20:00 CEST). On Sunday, May 24, the adidas NextGen EuroLeague Championship Game will tip off at 18:00 local time (17:00 CEST), while the season will culminate with the Championship Game at 21:00 local time (20:00 CEST), when a new EuroLeague champion will be crowned.

A third ticket sales window will open on Tuesday, March 17, at 10:00 CET, offering another opportunity for fans to secure their seats. The final sales phase will launch on Monday, April 20, at 10:00 CEST, positioned between the conclusion of the Regular Season and the start of the Play-In Showdown, with 10 teams still in contention for a place in the Final Four.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

articleMetadata.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
articleMetadata.description: Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

heroMedia.title: Italian Cup: Armoni Brooks powers Milan to the finals

content.text:
Armoni Brooks scored 27 points and came up clutch time after time in the final minutes of the game to lead EA7 Emporio Armani Milan past Germani Brescia, 106-102, and into the Italian Cup finals where it will play against Bertram Derthona Tortona which beat Virtus Bologna 84-78,

Brooks scored 22 points in the final 13 minutes of the game, and his run started when Milan needed him the most, down 65-70 late in the third quarter.

In the final 2:20 of the third, Brooks hit three triples, including a last-second shot to give Milan 76-72 lead. He added 5 more points to open 83-76 lead early in the fourth. Shavon Shields hit a three-pointer, then tossed up an alley-oop pass for Josh Nebo’s dunk and 92-84, but Brescia rallied scoring 7 consecutive points.

But Brooks then scored a tough runner, then a three-pointer, then an ankle-breaking step-back jumper to open 99-93 lead. A triple from Shileds sealed the deal and secured Milan’s place in the final where it will play either Tortona or Virtus Bologna on Sunday.

Shields and Zach LeDay scored 17 apiece, while Marko Guduric had 15 points in a win. Amadeo Della Valle scored 29 and C.J. Massinburg 28 points for Brescia.

Later on Saturday, Tortona beat Virtus 84-78, The key for Tortona was an 11-0 run to open the fourth quarter as Prentiss Hubb scored 20 and Justin Gorham 18 points. Matthew Morgan led Virtus with 23 points, and Saliou Niang scored 13.

articleMetadata.title: Italian Cup: Armoni Brooks powers Milan to the finals

heroMedia.title: Italian Cup: Virtus rallies to secure semifinal spot
heroMedia.summary: A big second half allowed Virtus to lock up a showdown with Derthona

content.text:
Virtus Bologna turned a slow start into a statement second-half performance, defeating Napoli Basket 83-73 in the semifinals of the Italian Cup. After trailing at halftime, Virtus flipped the momentum with a dominant third quarter and closed strong to book a spot in the next round, where it will face Derthona Basket.

Carsen Edwards led the way with 24 points, pacing a balanced offensive effort. Derrick Alston added 16 points, while Saliou Niang contributed 13. Momo Diouf anchored the paint with 10 points and 14 rebounds, and Luca Vildoza chipped in 5 points along with 7 assists to guide the offense.

Napoli came out sharper, taking the opening quarter 20-15 and extending its edge by winning the second 15-13 to carry a 35-28 lead into halftime. Virtus, however, responded with authority after the break. A 30-19 third quarter swung the game in Virtus's favor, turning a deficit into a four-point lead entering the final period.

Virtus maintained its momentum in the fourth, outscoring Napoli 25-19 to secure the 83-73 victory. Strong defensive stretches and efficient scoring down the stretch ensured the comeback was completed, sending Virtus into the semifinals with confidence.

articleMetadata.title: Italian Cup: Virtus rallies to secure semifinal spot
articleMetadata.description: Virtus Bologna turned a slow start into a statement second-half performance, defeating Napoli Basket 83-73 in the semifinals of the Italian Cup. After trailing at halftime, Virtus flipped the momentum with a dominant third quarter and closed strong to book a spot in the next round, where it will face Derthona Basket.
